intro: Calystegia binghamiae is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family, known for its climbing habit and trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Calystegia binghamiae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Calystegia binghamiae is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
